Worth monitoring if they post a Director-level BD role in this vertical.

watch_list.why: EO analytics for climate + environment; Charlottesville; commercial data licensing; small team at inflection point.
watch_list.roast: Charlottesville startup that somehow convinced VCs that 'EO analytics for climate' is a business model and not just a grad student's thesis title. They need someone who can actually sell this stuff to people who don't already have a PhD. That someone might be you, you beautiful disaster.

watch_list.why: EO data marketplace aggregating satellite tasking; commercial partnerships building distribution layer.
watch_list.roast: Australian EO marketplace that aggregates satellite data the way Uber aggregates taxi drivers — by taking a cut and hoping nobody notices. They're building the Amazon of satellite imagery and they need a BD person who's actually closed a deal bigger than a Series A press release.

watch_list.why: Leading carbon registry; digital MRV + satellite verification growing fast.
watch_list.roast: The carbon credit registry that every climate bro name-drops at conferences. They're going digital MRV and satellite verification, which means they need someone who speaks both 'carbon market theater' and 'actual remote sensing.' You literally wrote regulations. This is your kind of circus.

watch_list.why: Satellite-based carbon + crop monitoring; EUDR compliance angle; commercial data programs.
watch_list.roast: They monitor crops from space and sell carbon credits to farmers. It's like if your Norwegian Arctic career had a baby with a Nebraska cornfield. EUDR compliance is their new thing — nothing says 'exciting Tuesday' like European deforestation regulations.

watch_list.why: Drone data marketplace brokering aerial + EO data globally; commercial partnerships model.
watch_list.roast: Swedish drone data marketplace. Think Airbnb but for drone pilots. They broker aerial data globally and need someone who can sell 'we'll fly a drone over your stuff' to enterprise clients without it sounding vaguely threatening.

watch_list.why: IoT + AIS data from LEO constellation; maritime tracking; growing commercial verticals.
watch_list.roast: Spanish IoT constellation company doing maritime tracking from LEO. They're basically putting cell towers in space for boats. If you ever wanted to explain satellite AIS data to a shipping executive who thinks GPS is witchcraft, this is your moment.

watch_list.why: Hyperspectral satellite startup; commercial partnerships for precision agriculture.
watch_list.roast: Indian hyperspectral satellite startup that raised $36M to take pictures of Earth in 150 colors instead of the usual 4. They need someone who can explain why 150 colors is worth $36M to customers who just want to know if their crops are dying.

watch_list.why: Satellite-based analytics for water utilities, dams, forests. Series A, UK-based. No BD roles open now but strong domain overlap.
watch_list.roast: UK company using satellites to stare at dams and water pipes. Series A, profitable-ish, and desperately in need of someone who can sell 'we watch your infrastructure rot from space' as a value proposition rather than a threat.

watch_list.why: Norwegian drone + satellite company from Andoya Space ecosystem. ESA-funded. KSAT-adjacent. Very small, monitor for growth.
watch_list.roast: Norwegian drone company from the Andøya Space ecosystem. ESA-funded. KSAT-adjacent. This is basically your ex-colleagues starting a company without you. You could either be bitter about it or go run their commercial side. Both are valid.

watch_list.why: Stratospheric balloon remote sensing for wildfire and mapping. Denver-based. No BD roles now, mostly engineering.
watch_list.roast: Denver startup that flies stratospheric balloons for wildfire mapping. It's like Google Loon had a more practical younger sibling. They need BD help and they're in your time zone, which at this point in the search feels like a luxury.

watch_list.why: Paul would apply for the right role here. Watch for Director/VP of Commercial, Head of BD, VP of Sales, or revenue-leadership openings. PMM (2026-04 posting) is not the seat; rejected for AE 2026-03-31. Comp floor $165K.
watch_list.roast: The company that bought UrtheCast's assets and is trying to make daily global imagery a thing. They're the 'we have a constellation' company that actually needs someone to figure out who wants to buy daily pictures of the entire planet. Spoiler: everyone, if you pitch it right.

watch_list.why: Heat pump manufacturer, climate-tech darling, DCVC-backed. Director-level sales role surfaced in audit.
watch_list.roast: They make heat pumps and somehow raised venture capital for it. Climate tech's answer to 'what if we made HVAC sexy?' They need a Strategic Sales Director for the West Coast, and honestly, if you can sell satellite data to oil companies in Tromsø, you can sell heat pumps to Californians who are already convinced.

watch_list.why: Swappable battery containers for cargo ships. Ocean + climate crossover. Growth Manager role spotted.
watch_list.roast: They want to put giant batteries on cargo ships. Like Tesla but for the ocean and somehow even more ambitious. Maritime decarbonization is a space where your ocean-adjacent KSAT background actually matters, and 'Growth Manager' is just 'BD person' wearing a hoodie.

watch_list.why: Forest carbon verification via satellite and AI. Your MRV and remote sensing background is a direct hit.
watch_list.roast: Silicon Valley's attempt to save the rainforest using LiDAR and machine learning. They verify carbon credits by staring at trees from space, which is basically what you've been training for your entire career without knowing it. They're well-funded and perpetually hiring BD people who can explain remote sensing to carbon market buyers without putting them to sleep.

watch_list.why: Building weather and climate satellites. Commercial partnerships team would be a fit.
watch_list.roast: Named after a subatomic particle, because nothing says 'we're approachable' like physics jargon. They're building climate monitoring satellites and actually shipping hardware, which in the NewSpace world makes them practically blue chip. They need people who understand both the satellite and the customer, which is a Venn diagram with about six people in the overlap. You're one of them.

watch_list.why: Thermal infrared satellite data for agriculture and water. Niche EO play with commercial potential.
watch_list.roast: They take the Earth's temperature from space. Literally. Thermal infrared for crop water stress, which sounds boring until you realize every ag company on the planet will pay for this in five years. Early-stage, scrappy, and they probably need someone who can sell 'we measure how thirsty your corn is' to executives.

watch_list.why: The Moody's of carbon credits. Rates quality of offsets using satellite data. Your MRV background is gold here.
watch_list.roast: They rate carbon credits the way Moody's rates bonds — except the thing they're rating is whether a tree is actually still standing. London-based but remote-friendly, and they've raised enough money to suggest this isn't just a climate conference fever dream. Your satellite verification background is catnip for these people.

watch_list.why: Satellite-based vegetation management for power utilities. Climate resilience meets boring infrastructure money.
watch_list.roast: They use satellites to tell power companies which trees are about to fall on their power lines. It's the most boring-sounding business that makes the most obvious sense. Utilities have unlimited budgets for 'don't let California catch fire again' and Overstory is cashing those checks. Amsterdam-based, remote-possible, and the kind of company where your EO background meets actual recurring revenue.

watch_list.why: Science-based carbon removal verification. Like Verra but for permanent removal, not offsets.
watch_list.roast: Imagine Verra but run by people who think Verra is too easy on carbon credits. They verify permanent carbon removal — the 'we actually buried it underground' kind, not the 'we planted a tree and hoped for the best' kind. Backed by serious money and hiring aggressively. Your regulatory + MRV background is disturbingly relevant.

watch_list.why: The OG daily imagery company. Always hiring. You probably know people there already.
watch_list.roast: The granddaddy of NewSpace EO. They photograph the entire Earth every day and have been trying to figure out how to make money from it since 2010. Public company now, which means they have actual revenue targets instead of just vibes. You've probably met half their BD team at conferences. The question isn't whether you'd fit — it's whether they have a role that matches your ego. They do.

watch_list.why: Above-ground biomass mapping from satellite data. Finnish company, small, very niche.
watch_list.roast: Finnish company that measures how much carbon is stored in trees by looking at them from space. It's like a more specific version of your entire career. They're small enough that joining would mean you ARE the commercial team, which is either terrifying or exactly what you want on a Tuesday.

watch_list.why: Climate risk analytics for assets and supply chains. EO-adjacent, enterprise SaaS.
watch_list.roast: They tell companies which of their buildings will be underwater in 20 years. Climate risk analytics for the 'oh god we need to disclose this to investors now' crowd. It's EO-adjacent enterprise SaaS, which means the sales cycles are long, the contracts are fat, and they need someone who won't flinch when a CFO asks 'but how accurate is your flood model, really?' You've had worse conversations in Tromsø.

watch_list.why: Constellation of weather balloons collecting atmospheric data. Paul's NewSpace + climate crossover.
watch_list.roast: They launch weather balloons. Hundreds of them. Into the stratosphere. To collect atmospheric data that NOAA can't get any other way. It sounds like a science fair project that accidentally became a real company, and it kind of is, except they're backed by Khosla and actually selling data to national weather services. Not glamorous, but neither is Taos in mud season.

watch_list.why: Drone-based ecosystem restoration and monitoring. Oxford spinout, scaling commercially.
watch_list.roast: Oxford spinout that plants trees with drones and monitors them with satellites. It's the full circle of 'technology will save us' optimism, and honestly, they might be right. They're scaling commercially and need people who can sell 'we'll restore your ecosystem for you' without it sounding like a Greenpeace pitch.

watch_list.why: Weather intelligence platform with own radar satellites. Commercial BD team is substantial.
watch_list.roast: They built their own weather radar satellites because apparently the existing ones weren't good enough. Israeli founders, Boston HQ, massive commercial ambitions. They want to be the Bloomberg Terminal of weather. Their BD team is real and they sell to everyone from airlines to the US Air Force. Your satellite + enterprise sales background would land well, plus they're a Patriots-friendly commute if hybrid.

watch_list.why: Satellite-based ag MRV platform. Soil carbon, sustainability reporting, EUDR. Direct domain overlap.
watch_list.roast: They measure soil carbon from space and sell sustainability reports to food companies who need to prove they're not destroying the planet. EUDR compliance is their growth engine, which means European bureaucracy is literally funding their Series B. Your carbon market + satellite background is such a perfect fit it's almost suspicious.

morning_briefs.result: Open Cosmos Sales Lead, EO Data (72.5) - 1 deep dive. 1 notable (Open Cosmos Data CSM 51.0). 2 excluded (Rocket Lab TS/SCI, Planet Defence ME). 20 others scored under 40 (supply chain, compliance, HR tech, engineering, field survey, GIS dev roles). Digest skewed toward non-commercial functions. Open Cosmos was previously archived at 57.0; deep dive research upgraded significantly.

Fugro PCM Climate & Nature (66.5) + Fugro Commercial Manager (60.5) — 1 combined deep dive. 5 notables (Satlantis 59.5, Slingshot APAC 56.5, ElectraSteel Head of Sales 50.0, NatureMetrics Interim Marketing 45.0, Medable SD BD 44.5). 2 excluded (Maxar NRO Director, Maxar Technical Targeter — defense). Fugro is strongest domain match in this search.
Thin day - 5 excluded (UP42, Kpler, 3x defense), 1 scored 40+ (Patch 46.5 AE, pass), 0 deep dives. Digest heavy on engineering, HR, supply chain, junior roles.
EarthRanger (79) + Umbra Space (75.5) + Crusoe Energy (62) | Descartes Singapore added
